summary_one_line: Input must carry the ICMPv6 next-header value 0x3a in the IPv6 protocol field so the switch dispatches to case kProtoIcmp6.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The IPv6 next-header / protocol byte fed to HandlePayload's switch (aIpProto) must equal kProtoIcmp6 (0x3a = 58). Only that exact value steers the second switch (line 953) to the blocked case at line 972; kProtoUdp and other values fall to the already-covered UDP/default arms.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds embed the literal byte 0x3a at the protocol position: seed 1 has '10 3a' at offsets 0x06-0x07 and seeds 2-5 carry '10 3a' / '3a' at the inner header (offset 0x29 shows 3a concentrated x8 in W). The DIVERGENT BRANCHES table shows W takes the true side of case kProtoIcmp6 (T=10 F=20) at L972 while never resolving UDP-only paths, matching an exact-match dispatch on 0x3a.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds never place 0x3a at the protocol field: the byte diff at the protocol-bearing offsets shows L bytes dominated by 0x44/0x30/0xff runs (e.g. offset 0x29 L = '00 29 30', no 3a), so the second switch always lands on the UDP/default arm and L records T=0 F=20 at case kProtoIcmp6 (line 972) — the true side is never reached in 8/10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer recording the switch comparison (aIpProto == kProtoIcmp6), then substitutes the constant operand 0x3a directly into the input at the matching offset, planting the exact protocol byte that naive's blind havoc/token stack only stumbles onto 2/10 times.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the kProtoIcmp6 case arm of a protocol switch in HandlePayload, true side globally blocked.
The switch on aIpProto selects the ICMP6 arm only for that exact protocol value; the UDP/default arms are covered by both roles.
Winner seeds carry the literal ICMPv6 protocol byte 0x3a in the header region.
At the protocol-bearing inner offset 0x29 the winner concentrates byte 0x3a (x8) while the loser never carries it.
The winner resolves the kProtoIcmp6 true side while the loser never does, per the divergent-branch table.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 972  T=10 F=20  T=0 F=20  case kProtoIcmp6:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has only the havoc + token stack with no I2S, so it must blindly produce 0x3a.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog resolves the branch 10/10 while naive blocks 8/10, a prob_div of 0.80.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 13333 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 4f1af37e9d5867a4` and observed one I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 25 (ancestor 4578ba613b6d1a35). A second winning seed 3280d8d2781fbeb1 shares the same dash-row ancestor at depth 19, corroborating an I2S contribution in the winning lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose ICMP6 arm is reached when the protocol byte is any value other than 0x3a (e.g. a range/inequality test rather than an exact == kProtoIcmp6 dispatch) — that would show the divergence is not driven by exact-constant I2S substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact input offset of the protocol byte consumed by HandlePayload's switch is inferred from byte-diff concentration of 0x3a rather than confirmed by a dataflow trace from the fuzz harness to aIpProto.
